tools.name: search_das
tools.description: Search the public database of California District Attorneys with documented misconduct or controversy. Returns name, county, in-office date, misconduct type, description, source URL.

tools.name: get_da
tools.description: Get a full DA record by name (exact or close partial match).

tools.name: search_defenders
tools.description: Search the public database of California public defenders with documented case outcomes, failures, or systemic-context entries. Includes Bar number, county, office, score.

tools.name: get_defender
tools.description: Get a full defender record by Bar number id or name.

tools.name: search_judges
tools.description: Search current California judges by name and/or court type (e.g., "superior", "appeal", "supreme"). Returns name + court type pairs.

tools.name: search_officers
tools.description: Search the public database of California law enforcement officers with documented misconduct or decertification. Includes name, agency, basis.

tools.name: get_officer
tools.description: Get a full officer record by name (with optional agency filter for disambiguation).

tools.name: search_cjp
tools.description: Search the California Commission on Judicial Performance (CJP) public-discipline records — judges censured, admonished, or removed for misconduct. Source: cjp.ca.gov public decisions.

tools.name: search_cjp_documents
tools.description: Semantic search over the full text of CJP public-discipline decisions (250 PDFs ingested). Use this for topic questions ("racial bias", "drug-related misconduct", "ex parte communications") or when you need passages, not just summary records. Returns matching passages with citations. Distinct from s

tools.name: search_capost
tools.description: Search the California POST decertification database — law enforcement officers stripped of their POST certification. Source: post.ca.gov public records.

tools.name: list_databases
tools.description: List all public databases exposed by this MCP server with their metadata (entry counts, last updated, descriptions).

tools.name: top_das
tools.description: Return the top-N California DAs by editorial severity score (descending). Use for "worst DA" / "most-disciplined DA" / ranking questions. Optional county filter. Only manually-scored records are returned.

tools.name: top_judges
tools.description: Return the top-N California judges by CJP discipline severity (removal > censure > admonishment, descending). Use for "worst judge" / "most-disciplined judge" / ranking questions.

tools.name: top_officers
tools.description: Return the top-N California law enforcement officers by editorial severity score (descending). Use for "worst officer" / "most-disciplined officer" / ranking questions. Optional agency filter.

tools.name: list_pages
tools.description: Return the canonical list of pages on cajusticewatch.com — slug, URL, label, and purpose. Use this when the user asks about features/pages/tools of the site, OR when you need to recommend a page, OR before saying "I do not have access to X" — the page may actually exist.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: search_das, search_capost, list_pages · Private data: search_das, get_da, search_defenders, get_defender, search_officers, get_officer, search_cjp, search_cjp_documents, search_capost, top_das · Public sink: search_capost.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: search_das, search_capost, list_pages · Private data: search_das, get_da, search_defenders, get_defender, search_officers, get_officer, search_cjp, search_cjp_documents, search_capost, top_das · Public sink: search_capost

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
